title: 2D Art Explorers – Mixed Media Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Welcome to 2D Art Explorers, where young artists discover the magic of mark-making, color, and visual tricks that make your eyes do a double-take! This hands-on camp takes campers on a creative journey through four exciting disciplines — all using classic art tools and materials that artists have relied on for centuries.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

title: Sewing Basics – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Come explore the wonderful world of textiles arts! We will dive into learning to use a sewing machine. Participants will learn basic sewing techniques. Discover the tactile joy of working with colorful fabrics. We will complete projects to showcase your new sewing skills.

title: Build Your Own Skateboard – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Build your dream skateboard at our summer camp! Learn woodworking skills and make a custom board that stands out with your unique laser cut designs., Using a vacuum press and multiple layers of veneer, create a custom board with a kick . You will learn wood equipment safety and use of band saws, panel saw, router, sanders as well as learn and use laser cutters. Finish up by laser etching your design onto your deck then stain, seal and assemble trucks, wheels and bearings. Your end product will be a high-end custom skateboard!

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Wonder Circle Mythic Art & Nature Journey Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Welcome to 2D Art Explorers, where young artists discover the magic of mark-making, color, and visual tricks that make your eyes do a double-take! This hands-on camp takes campers on a creative journey through four exciting disciplines — all using classic art tools and materials that artists have relied on for centuries.

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show
Cosplay Curious: Teen Mixed Media Art Club
description: Come as you are. Make something bold. Meet other creative teens., This bi-monthly Teen Art Club is a welcoming, inclusive creative meetup for youth ages 14–19 who are interested in art, cosplay, mixed media, and hands-on making. Facilitated by artist Clara Frawley, this group blends art-making, skill-sharing, and community connection in a relaxed, supportive environment.

title: 2D Art Explorers – Mixed Media Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Welcome to 2D Art Explorers, where young artists discover the magic of mark-making, color, and visual tricks that make your eyes do a double-take! This hands-on camp takes campers on a creative journey through four exciting disciplines — all using classic art tools and materials that artists have relied on for centuries.

title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Beaucoup Chapeaux
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., Beaucoup Chapeaux is an acoustic world-fusion quartet noted for their extraordinary, eclectic repertoire that effortlessly blends Parisian cabaret, Balkan folk, Italian cafe tunes, Celtic airs, and hot club jazz alongside originals. Maggie McKaig: vocals accordion and guitar; Luke Wilson: strings, tenor guitar, dobro, and plectrum banjo; Murray Campbell: violin, oboe, and cor anglais; Randy McKean: clarinet, bass clarinet, and saxophone.

title: Intro to Macrame
description: Come relax, unwind, and explore the beautiful, hands-on art of macramé! In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll create your own macramé wall hanging while learning the history and techniques behind this timeless craft. Students will learn a variety of basic knots and patterns to create a beautiful wall hanging. This workshop is suitable for complete beginners to the art of macrame or anyone wanting a refresher course!

title: Community conversation on following your calling
description: Join the Village Project for an ongoing conversation exploring purpose, service, and meaningful participation in community life. Whether you feel drawn to caring for the environment, mentoring youth, supporting elders, or serving in other ways, there is a place for you to participate within the 12 sectors of community needs we will explore together on June 25th. To learn more and stay in touch visit,, www.facebook.clm…

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show

THURSDAY NIGHT MARKET SUMMER SERIES ─ Grass Valley’s Favorite Summer Tradition is Back! Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley!
description: Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley! Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music., Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to the farm fresh produce at the Certified Farmers' Market and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

title: Nevada County Playwrights Collective Performance
description: Performances: June 25th-28th of new plays written and performed by and for the Nevada County community.

title: Nevada City Film Festival
description: The Nevada City Film Festival returns for its 26th edition — June, 26–28, 2026 — and this year, it's going deeper., Part festival, part think tank, part creative gathering, NCFF 2026, brings together filmmakers, artists, and audiences to watch extraordinary films, exchange ideas, and reimagine what cinema can be., The program features 50+ films across 7 curated short film programs, a special spotlight on animation — including award-winning work from Sundance, Cannes, TIFF, and beyond — creative cohorts with working artists and industry leaders, and an Artist Spotlight with world-renowned light painter Dariustwin. Thursday night kicks off with an intimate fundraising screening of CATCH, a locally-made film from Emmy-nominated director Jared Callahan. Saturday morning brings a free family program featuring a creature craft workshop with a Jim Henson's, Creature Shop fabricator and a screening of Where the Wild Things Are., Named one of the Top 50 Film Festivals in the World by Moviemaker Magazine six times, NCFF has been a celebrated home for fiercely independent cinema since 2001., Great cinema. Real conversation. One unforgettable weekend.

title: Cars and coffee grass valley
description: Cars and coffee grass valley is a weekly car event at daily donuts in the Mcknight crossing shopping center every saturday morning from 7 to 10

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Sierra Storytelling Festival
description: In a time of six-lane freeways, high-speed internet and split-second decisions, the Sierra Storytelling Festival steps out of a busy schedule to take a breath and celebrate in a style reminiscent of our ancestors’ gathering around a fire. A group of dedicated volunteers provides a festival to hear stories, to build culture, to review traditions., Stories not only entertain us but they reach inside to our humanity and bind us together, open our minds and help us imagine a better future., For 39 years the Sierra Storytelling Festival has invited a diverse group of Tellers at the top of their game, from the far reaches of the world, to gather together for one magical weekend of tales, lessons and anecdotes.

title: Make a Tiny Grief Book
description: Make a Tiny Grief Book – A Book Arts Workshop

title: Live theatrical performance of Butterfly Home
description: Live Theatrical Performance of Butterfly Home, Saturday, June 27, 9am – 10am, Meet at the Litton Trail trailhead on Sierra College Drive in Grass Valley. The performance will move along the paved section of trail., What connects us to an iridescent milkweed puff glinting in the breeze? Have we lost touch with the pulse of our own nature? Join us for a guided walk along the Litton Trail and experience Butterfly Home — a live, immersive theatrical performance unfolding throughout the forest. From one of the creators of “Project Wild Edges,” this moving outdoor production explores disconnection, transformation, and reclamation through the lens of the monarch butterfly’s metamorphosis., This FREE live performance takes place along the trail itself — no ticket required. Simply arrive at the trailhead ready to walk, witness, and be guided through the experience as the story unfolds around you in nature.

title: Spanish story time
description: Monthly bilingual story time with Reyna Sanchez-Lopez. Enjoy lively books and activities to introduce children to the wonders of reading and books.

title: Queer Burlesque & Dance Party
description: Queer Burlesque & Dance Party , Taste the Rainbow with Iridescent Indulgence Burlesque 🌈, This event brings together local queer performers, DJs, and friends of KVMR/KVMRx for a night of bold art, movement, and community. Proceeds to benefit KVMR and KVMRx Community Radio;, support independent community radio and the diverse voices heard on our airwaves., End Pride Month with a Bang!

title: Make your own Hand-Carved Wooden Spoon
description: Hand-Carved Wooden – Spoons and Spatulas (see tickets for project), 3 hours class – , There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece., This class is perfect for anyone curious about traditional woodworking or wanting to slow down and experience the rhythm of working with hand tools. No prior experience is necessary — just bring your curiosity and a willingness to learn., By the end of class, you’ll leave with a hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula and the foundational skills to continue carving on your own., What You’ll Create , One hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula (depending on the class ticket you choose), What You’ll Learn, • Safe and proper use of an axe, • Knife carving techniques, • How to use a scoop knife for hollowing, • Foundational greenwood carving skills, Experience Level, Beginners— No experience needed, Ages, Adults, and Teens 13+ may participate when accompanied by an adult., (The accompanying adult may choose to observe or purchase a ticket to participate.), All materials and tools are provided.

title: Pride Family Fest
description: You are invited to the 5th Annual Pride Family Fest on June 28th (11–3pm) at the Condon Park Picnic Pavilion. This all-ages, multi-generational event celebrates LGBTQ+ families in all their beautiful diversity. We’ll have hotdogs (vegan and gluten-free options too), cake, games, activities, and art. Please bring a potluck side to share!, Sign up to volunteer here: www.signupgenius.com…

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: The Creative Labyrinth: Nature & Creativity Bookmaking
description: This immersive workshop invites participants into bookmaking as a creative and reflective journey., Using creative folded book structures, natural materials, and guided creativity practices, participants are introduced to a process that blends hands-on making with personal exploration. Each book becomes more than an object—it becomes a container for thought, intuition, and meaning., Rather than focusing on traditional bookbinding techniques, this workshop emphasizes accessible folded forms that allow for both completion and creative freedom. Participants will create three unique handmade books while exploring a deeper connection to their own creative voice.

title: Ceramics – Garden Art
description: Come in and make some Ceramic Garden Art with Bob Davis, Dive into the world of ceramics with Bob Davis in our Ceramic Garden Art class—no experience needed! Over a 3-class series, unleash your creativity as you learn to hand-build delightful Bird Houses, Garden Totems, or Bat Houses. Master the art of slab and coil building, explore pressing designs into clay, and get introduced to various glazing techniques. This workshop promises fun and inventive sessions where your imagination can soar:Making Garden Art with Clay: A 3-hour session of demonstrations and hands-on crafting., Decorating and Underglazing: Another 3-hour session to add color and life to your pieces, followed by bisque firing., Final Glazing: A 3-hour session to complete your projects with glazing and under-glazing techniques., Ages 16+, Price: $162 + $38 materials, Pre-registration required., Class 4 minimum/8 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet minimum, class will be rescheduled)

title: Nevada County Playwrights Collective Performance
description: Performances: June 25th-28th of new plays written and performed by and for the Nevada County community.

title: Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert
description: Under the direction of Cheryl Woldseth, the 60-member Nevada County Concert Band invites you to join us for the first of our three Pioneer Park Picnic Pops concerts. Make it a family tradition to enjoy a picnic concert with the Nevada County Concert Band! Expect a lively program, including toe-tapping marches, iconic Broadway hits, contemporary tunes, and much more. Bring your friends, family, neighbors, and clubs to the park for a fun-filled evening of music with the official Nevada County band! The concert is free, open to the public, and accessible to all, making it a perfect outing for families. Leashed dogs are welcome. Pack a picnic dinner or treat yourself to hot dogs and ice cream from our vendors. Don't forget your lawn chair or blanket, and settle in for a delightful evening of music at the park.

title: Glass Flameworking Summer Camp Level 2
description: Prerequisite: For returning students who’ve completed a Curious Forge Youth Flameworking Camp or Class, Take your glass skills to the next level! In this Level 2 workshop, students will build on their torch safety and technique knowledge to create exciting new projects like flower implosions, marbles, small sculptures, mini hedgehogs, and more!, Led by experienced instructors, this hands-on class is both fun and educational — all in a supportive, creativity-filled environment where every young maker can shine., Ages 12+, 4-Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday, 1:00 to 4:00 PM each day, $275, Class 4 minimum/6 maximum participants

title: Nevada City Odd Fellows Open Mic
description: The most relaxed, most welcoming open mic in Western Nevada County

title: Ruthie Foster
description: Ruthie Foster brings her Grammy-nominated blend of blues, gospel, soul, and folk to the Marisa Funk Theater at The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley on Monday, June 29. Raised in Texas and shaped by church music and classic R&B, Foster has built an international following through powerful live performances and deeply personal songwriting. Her voice fills a room with warmth and conviction — and an intimate theater is exactly where it hits hardest. This is roots music at its most honest., Doors 6:00 PM | Show 7:00 PM

title: Tuesday Morning Ceramics – Hand-Building Techniques
description: Tuesday Morning Ceramics and Pottery Class with Pamela Montalbano, 6 class series, Tuesday Mornings 10:30 am – 12:30 pm, Price: $271 Public ($216 for 6 classes + $55 materials), Discover your creativity, engage your mind, and come play with clay! In this Ceramics and Pottery Class, we will use hand-building techniques to make functional and non-functional ceramics and pottery projects using slabs and coils. Clay, Glazing, and firings are included in the materials fee. Participants can sign up for additional Tuesday Morning classes each month if they wish to continue their ceramics adventure., We will be hand-building and then every third class will be a glazing class. At least 2 projects will be made in this series., Class 5 minimum/8 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet the minimum, the class will be rescheduled)

title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - The Mountain Road Ceilidh Band
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Mountain Road Ceilidh Band is a highly sought-after, regional Celtic music ensemble based in Nevada County specializing in bringing the energetic, toe-tapping traditions of Irish and Scottish music to community gatherings, festivals, and folk dances. Shawn Prescott: Flute, guitar, and bodhrán; Guy Prudhomme: Guitar; Shawn Prudhomme: Fiddle; Dave Riddles: Bagpipes

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: THURSDAY NIGHT MARKET SUMMER SERIES ─ Grass Valley’s Favorite Summer Tradition is Back! Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley!
description: Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley! Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music., Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to the farm fresh produce at the Certified Farmers' Market and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

description: Northwoods Clubhouse, Tahoe Donner

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Nevada County Concert Band - Independence Day Celebration
venue_name: Downtown curbside across from the National Exchange Hotel (Broad St).
description: Under the direction of Cheryl Woldseth, the 60-member Nevada County Concert Band invites you to join us, as the band joins for the Independence Day celebrations. The Nevada County Concert Band (NCCB) brings back to life an era in American history when local band concerts were a grand social occasion. Come join us and wave a flag at the curbside with the band at the Independence Day Parade. Our local volunteer musicians will entertain you with a fine selection of family-friendly music.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: 4th of July • STAR-SPANGLED BASH! Dance Party on the Plaza
description: Celebrate 250 Years of Independence — Dancing in the Streets! , Get ready, Grass Valley — this year we’re turning up the red, white, and blue for a once-in-a-generation celebration! Join us on the Mill Street Plaza from 6:00 to 9:00 p.m. as we honor America’s 250th anniversary with an evening bursting with music, flavor, and hometown pride., Dance your way through the decades to the high-energy sounds of the 4th of July dance band, bringing the spirit of celebration to life! Savor a festive spread of mouthwatering street food, sip on local beer, wine, sparkling cider, and handcrafted cocktails, and soak in the joy of community under the summer sky. , From classic Independence Day vibes to a historic milestone celebration, this is your invitation to laugh, dance, and celebrate 250 years of freedom — together., ✨ Come for the music, stay for the memories — let’s make history on Mill Street!

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight

title: Silly Sculptures & Fun Figures Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Join us for a colorful, hands-on adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining!, Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium for kids of all ages to explore their creativity. In this fun and flexible workshop, young makers will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life – from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and imaginative keepsakes.. Children are encouraged to use their imaginations and create whatever they like.

title: Hand Carving Wooden Spoons Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Description, Come experience the timeless craft of carving and discover the joy of making something both simple and enduring., There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece.

title: Glass Fusing & Flameworking Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: This one-of-a-kind camp introduces young artists to two captivating glass art techniques — glass fusing and glass flameworking with a torch — giving campers a rare opportunity to work with real glass-studio tools and bring home stunning, handmade pieces they’ll be proud to display.
